title: MLKEM support
body:
```skopeo
> skopeo -v
< skopeo version 1.20.0
> skopeo inspect docker://example.example.com/library/fedora:43
< FATA[0000] Error parsing image name "docker://example.example.com/library/fedora:43": pinging container registry example.example.com: Get "https://example.example.com/v2/": remote error: tls: handshake failure
```

```nginx
nginx version: nginx/1.29.3
built by gcc 15.2.1 20251022 (Red Hat 15.2.1-3) (GCC)
built with OpenSSL 3.5.4 30 Sep 2025
```

```nginx
ssl_protocols TLSv1.3;
ssl_ecdh_curve 'X25519MLKEM768';
ssl_ciphers ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384;
ssl_conf_command Ciphersuites TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384;
ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on;
ssl_early_data off;
ssl_session_timeout 10m;
ssl_session_cache shared:SSL:10m;
ssl_session_tickets off;
```

```curl
curl 8.15.0 (x86_64-redhat-linux-gnu) libcurl/8.15.0 OpenSSL/3.5.4 zlib/1.3.1.zlib-ng brotli/1.1.0 libidn2/2.3.8 libpsl/0.21.5 libssh/0.11.3/openssl/zlib nghttp2/1.66.0 OpenLDAP/2.6.10
Release-Date: 2025-07-16
Protocols: dict file ftp ftps gopher gophers http https imap imaps ipfs ipns ldap ldaps mqtt pop3 pop3s rtsp scp sftp smb smbs smtp smtps telnet tftp ws wss
Features: alt-svc AsynchDNS brotli GSS-API HSTS HTTP2 HTTPS-proxy IDN IPv6 Kerberos Largefile libz NTLM PSL SPNEGO SSL threadsafe TLS-SRP UnixSockets
```

```os
macOS 26.0.1 (25A362)
Homebrew 4.6.20-50-g90ebad1
Homebrew/homebrew-core (git revision 413ca8981d1; last commit 2025-11-07)
Homebrew/homebrew-cask (git revision 92673f6be8d; last commit 2025-11-07)
```

---
# Attention

You MUST DISABLE all other curves in Nginx.

comments.body:
I cannot find why, Go 1.24 has enabled X25519MLKEM by default.

I think the main reason is

https://github.com/containers/image/blob/df7e80d2d19872b61f352a8a182ec934dc0c2346/docker/docker_client.go#L230

and 

https://github.com/containers/image/blob/df7e80d2d19872b61f352a8a182ec934dc0c2346/go.mod#L4

`image` is using Go 1.23.
Okay, I found Podman has this issue too. Maybe we should transfer this issue to `containers/image` repo.

Thanks for your report. Nowadays https://github.com/containers/container-libs would be the right location … but, at least reading the documentation, I don’t see that any of the linked code should make a difference.

These linking codes are all my guesses, and I'm also confused as to why this is happening. I apologize that I'm just trying to provide some help through these code links. But at least I can consistently reproduce the inability to connect successfully.

Looking at the implementing Go code, AFAICT setting `CipherSuites` doesn’t make any different to the TLS 1.3 version.

Is it possible that the server is configured in a way somehow incompatible with the Go TLS stack? I.e. does a minimal `net/http.Get` against that server work?

As a data point, with a server `openssl s_server` from macOS 26.1 (with ~no options set), and Skopeo built using `go1.25.3`, I see
`Shared groups: X25519MLKEM768:x25519:secp256r1:secp384r1:secp521r1` from the server, and a TLS connection is successfully established.

> X25519MLKEM768:x25519:secp256r1:secp384r1:secp521r1

Yeah, you should disable other curves, only rest a X25519MLKEM768.

`openssl s_server -key localhost-rsa.key -cert localhost-rsa.crt -port 52324 -group X25519MLKEM768` prints
`Shared groups: X25519MLKEM768` and also succeeds for me.

Strange, my openssl reports `no suitable key share`.
`OpenSSL 3.6.0 1 Oct 2025 (Library: OpenSSL 3.6.0 1 Oct 2025)`

`0008880102000000:error:0A000065:SSL routines:final_key_share:no suitable key share:ssl/statem/extensions.c:1471:`
Does `go version -v -m $(which skopeo)` show something unexpected?

Okay, that's not my build. Are the packages from Homebrew officially provided?
Maybe I should open an issue in Homebrew?

The Homebrew packages are not routinely maintained by us as upstream Skopeo maintainers.

I also can’t see any explicit code to disable MLKEM in https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/fe380f620295bad7ea81c1c7928e11d1a492e12f/Formula/s/skopeo.rb , or, indeed, anywhere in that repo. I’m mildly curious to learn why it is disabled and where, but it’s not something I can spend too much time on.

I don’t think this is an issue with `org:containers`, especially Skopeo.

I have started a discussion here: https://github.com/orgs/Homebrew/discussions/6532 .

I can build Skopeo locally, but the result only shows:
```
build   DefaultGODEBUG=containermaxprocs=0,decoratemappings=0,tlssha1=1,updatemaxprocs=0,x509sha256skid=0
```

However, even using `--build-with-source` via Homebrew doesn’t produce the same result.

And I believe this is more related to Go itself.

Therefore, I am closing this issue here.

Thank you for your help, and sorry for bothering you.
No worries, there really seems to be a puzzle about the Homebrew behavior, and it’s useful to be aware of that. Thanks!
